_meta.note: Scholarship identity/eligibility is stable year to year and manually curated here. Deadlines are intentionally stored as an approximate window, not a precise date -- exact application dates for a given scholarship commonly shift by days to weeks between cycles even when the program itself is unchanged. Always tell students to confirm the exact current-cycle date on the official source_url before relying on it. Award amounts are as last verified and may change between cycles. Re-verify this list at least once per admissions cycle for defunct/discontinued programs.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US high school seniors (citizens/nationals/permanent residents/refugees/asylees); achievement-based across leadership, service, and community impact; no minimum GPA specified

scholarships.name: QuestBridge National College Match
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US high school seniors from low-income households (roughly under $65,000/yr for a family of four), top 5–10% of class, strong academics
scholarships.award_amount: Full four-year scholarship at partner colleges for matched students

scholarships.name: The Gates Scholarship
scholarships.organization: Gates Scholarship / Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizen/permanent resident, Pell-eligible high school senior, minimum 3.3 GPA, strong leadership; top 10% of class strongly preferred
scholarships.award_amount: Full cost of attendance (last-dollar), often $160,000+ over four years

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US high school juniors who take the PSAT/NMSQT and score in roughly the top 1% in their state; open to homeschool and overseas US citizen students
scholarships.deadline_window: Early October of senior year (semifinalist application)

scholarships.name: Dell Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school senior, minimum 2.4 GPA, demonstrated financial need, Pell Grant eligible, participation in an approved college-readiness program in grades 11–12
scholarships.award_amount: $20,000 (paid in annual installments, plus laptop and counseling support)

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger National Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US high school juniors/seniors who have faced and overcome significant financial and personal adversity, community service/extracurriculars, critical financial need

scholarships.name: Jack Kent Cooke Foundation College Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High-achieving high school seniors with financial need, minimum 3.75 unweighted GPA, household income generally under ~$95,000

scholarships.name: Ron Brown Scholar Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizen/permanent resident high school seniors; academic achievement, leadership, financial need, interest in public service/community engagement/entrepreneurship
scholarships.award_amount: $40,000 total ($10,000/year for four years, 20 scholars annually)

scholarships.name: Elks National Foundation Most Valuable Student Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizen high school seniors planning full-time enrollment in a four-year undergraduate program; merit-based, no Elks affiliation required

scholarships.name: Burger King Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors heading to college/vocational-trade program (or BK employees/dependents); minimum 3.5 GPA, work experience, leadership, financial need considered

scholarships.name: Foot Locker Scholar Athletes Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Graduating high school seniors involved in school/intramural/community sports, minimum 3.0 GPA, US citizen or permanent resident

scholarships.name: Davidson Fellows Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Students 18 or younger, US citizen or permanent resident, who have completed a significant original piece of work (STEM, literature, music, etc.)

scholarships.name: Equitable Excellence Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors planning full-time undergraduate enrollment, US citizens or legal residents, demonstrated ambition via a notable achievement
scholarships.award_amount: $10,000 (52 recipients); top 10 winners receive an additional $15,000

scholarships.name: Buick Achievers Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors/graduates or current undergrads planning full-time study in engineering, technology, or select design fields; US citizens/permanent residents of US or Puerto Rico
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $25,000, renewable up to four (or five for engineering) years

scholarships.name: Cameron Impact Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizen high school seniors, minimum 3.7 unweighted GPA, excellence in extracurriculars, leadership, community service/civic engagement
scholarships.award_amount: Full tuition, fees, and books for four years at any accredited US college (15 scholars annually)

scholarships.name: Society of Women Engineers Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Women entering their first year of undergraduate study pursuing an ABET-accredited engineering, technology, or computer science degree

scholarships.name: Hispanic Scholarship Fund (HSF Scholarship)
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Students of Hispanic heritage, minimum 3.0 GPA, US citizen/permanent resident/DACA recipient, planning full-time enrollment at an accredited four-year US university

scholarships.name: UNCF General Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: African American students enrolled or accepted at an accredited college/university, typically minimum 2.5 GPA; many programs need-based, some merit-based

scholarships.name: American Indian College Fund Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Enrolled member or descendant of an enrolled member of a state or federally recognized American Indian/Alaska Native tribe, minimum 2.0 GPA

scholarships.name: Coolidge Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizens/permanent residents, current high school juniors intending full-time undergraduate enrollment; highly selective
scholarships.award_amount: Full tuition, room, board, and expenses for four years plus a personal stipend

scholarships.name: Prudential Emerging Visionaries
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Ages 14–18, legal US or Puerto Rico residents, must have started a community-improvement or financial-literacy project by a set date
scholarships.award_amount: $5,000 (25 winners); Grand Prize winner receives an additional $10,000

scholarships.name: National FFA Scholarship Program
scholarships.organization: National FFA Organization / National FFA Foundation
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Active senior FFA members planning to attend an accredited post-secondary program

scholarships.name: National Honor Society Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school senior in good standing as an active NHS chapter member, planning to enroll at a US college/university

scholarships.name: VFW Voice of Democracy Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Students in grades 9–12 at a public, private, parochial, or home-study high school in the US, its territories, or possessions; must be 19 or younger
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $35,000 (national first place); other national awards $1,000–$21,000

scholarships.name: DAR America 250! Scholarship
scholarships.organization: National Society Daughters of the American Revolution
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizens, high school seniors or current students planning to attend an accredited US college; various programs by field of study
scholarships.award_amount: $25,000 (America 250! Scholarship, 10 students); other DAR scholarships vary

scholarships.name: Boys & Girls Clubs of America National Youth of the Year
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Active Boys & Girls Club members/teens nominated through local club and state-level competitions
scholarships.award_amount: $50,000 scholarship for the National Youth of the Year

scholarships.name: Simon Youth Foundation Community Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors who live/attend school within about 50 miles of a Simon Property Group mall, US legal residents

scholarships.name: Ayn Rand Institute Essay Contests
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Middle and high school students worldwide (ages 13+); essay-based on assigned novel (Anthem, The Fountainhead, Atlas Shrugged)
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $25,000 top prize; smaller awards from $25 upward across tiers

scholarships.name: DoSomething "It Pays to Do Good" Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Ages 13–25, US residents or US citizens living abroad; no essay, GPA, or recommendation requirements

scholarships.name: Point Foundation Flagship Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Full-time undergraduate students at accredited nonprofit US colleges who identify as LGBTQ+ or are strong allies, out/open about their identity
scholarships.award_amount: Financial award based on need, renewable up to four years

scholarships.name: P.E.O. STAR Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US or Canadian citizen/permanent resident women in their final year of high school, minimum 3.0 unweighted GPA, must be recommended by a local P.E.O. chapter

scholarships.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Students in their final year of secondary school in the US and its territories, regardless of citizenship status; requires an independent research project

scholarships.name: Toyota Scholarship Program
scholarships.organization: Toyota Motor North America / Scholarship America
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors/graduates or current undergrads with minimum 2.0 GPA; must be affiliated with a listed nonprofit partner organization
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $30,000 total ($7,500/year over four years)

scholarships.name: National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ) Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: African American students pursuing or majoring in journalism/media, enrolled in or admitted to an accredited four-year college/university

scholarships.name: Google Lime Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Students with a visible or invisible disability pursuing computer science, computer engineering, or a closely related technical field

scholarships.name: Golden Door Scholars
scholarships.eligibility_summary: DACA recipients or undocumented high school seniors/recent grads, or other underrepresented students willing to relocate; minimum 3.0 GPA, financial need
scholarships.award_amount: Varies (covers significant cost of attendance through partner colleges)

scholarships.name: Amazon Future Engineer Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors pursuing computer science or a closely related field, financial need considered
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $40,000 total ($10,000/year, renewable up to four years), plus a paid Amazon internship

scholarships.name: Discover Scholarship Sweepstakes
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US residents at least 16 years old, high school seniors or current college/graduate students enrolled at least half-time; no essay or GPA requirement

scholarships.name: Army ROTC Four-Year Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school students who have completed junior year, planning to enroll in a participating college with Army ROTC; commits to military service after graduation
scholarships.award_amount: Up to $69,000 in value plus a monthly stipend and book allowance
scholarships.deadline_window: Applications open summer, board review through early spring of senior year

scholarships.name: Air Force ROTC High School Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school students who have completed junior year, planning to attend a college with an Air Force ROTC detachment; commits to military service after graduation
scholarships.award_amount: Up to full tuition and fees plus a monthly stipend and book allowance

scholarships.name: Fisher House Foundation Scholarships for Military Children
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Dependent children of active-duty, reserve, or retired US military service members

scholarships.name: Folds of Honor Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Dependents and/or spouses of fallen or disabled US service members or first responders; must maintain minimum 2.0 GPA

scholarships.name: FBLA Distinguished Business Leader Scholarship (High School)
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school senior planning post-secondary business study, dues-paid FBLA member by March 1, achieved Advocate or Capstone level in FBLA's Business Achievement Awards program
scholarships.award_amount: Varies by year (funded by annual contributions to the scholarship fund)
scholarships.deadline_window: Spring (varies by year — confirm on FBLA's site)

scholarships.name: DECA Scholarships (High School)
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Active DECA high school membership required; business, marketing, finance, hospitality, or entrepreneurship coursework or career interest; strictly merit-based (leadership, DECA involvement, community service, academics) -- financial need not required
scholarships.award_amount: $1,000–$5,000 per scholarship (over $200,000 awarded annually across all DECA scholarships)
scholarships.deadline_window: Opens in fall, through mid-January (varies by specific scholarship)

scholarships.name: Scholastic Art & Writing Awards — Portfolio Gold Medal
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US (including territories/military bases) or Canadian students in grades 7–12, age 13+; graduating seniors submit a six-work portfolio (visual art or writing) with an accompanying artist/writer statement
scholarships.award_amount: $12,500 (Gold Medal Portfolio) or $2,000 (Silver Medal Portfolio); 16 Gold Medal Portfolios awarded annually (8 art, 8 writing)

scholarships.name: National YoungArts Foundation Award
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US citizens, permanent residents, or those legally able to work in the US, ages 15–18 or grades 10–12 (as of Dec 1), demonstrating excellence in visual, literary, or performing arts (classical music, dance, design, film, jazz, photography, theater, visual arts, voice, writing)
scholarships.award_amount: $250–$10,000 (select winners eligible for additional discretionary awards up to $25,000)

scholarships.name: PDK Educational Foundation Scholarship (Prospective Educators)
scholarships.organization: Phi Delta Kappa International (PDK) Educational Foundation
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Graduating high school seniors (Future Educators Association members preferred) intending to major in education and pursue a teaching career

scholarships.name: National Federation of the Blind Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Legally blind high school seniors or current college students residing in the US, DC, or Puerto Rico, planning full-time post-secondary study in the fall; disability-specific, awarded on academic achievement, service, and financial need
scholarships.award_amount: Multiple tiers; top awards $10,000, plus smaller scholarships

scholarships.name: Anne Ford Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Graduating high school senior with a documented learning disability (including ADHD when co-occurring with an LD), enrolling full-time in a four-year bachelor's degree program; disability-specific

scholarships.name: LEAGUE Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Self-identifying LGBTQ+ graduating high school seniors who are US citizens/legal residents, accepted at an accredited US college/university, minimum 3.0 GPA

scholarships.name: ThanksUSA Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Dependent children and spouses of active-duty, reserve, discharged, or retired US military service members who served after September 11, 2001; minimum 2.0 GPA; need-based, military/veteran family-specific

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Foundation First Generation HBCU Scholarship
scholarships.organization: Thurgood Marshall College Fund (TMCF) / The Coca-Cola Foundation
scholarships.eligibility_summary: Graduating high school seniors who are the first in their immediate family to attend college, planning to enroll full-time at a TMCF member HBCU/PBI; minimum 2.8 GPA, demonstrated financial need; need-based/first-gen-specific
scholarships.deadline_window: Varies by cycle (confirm on TMCF's site)

scholarships.name: Dollars for Scholars (Scholarship America)
scholarships.eligibility_summary: High school seniors in a community served by a local Dollars for Scholars chapter (400+ chapters nationwide); eligibility, award size, and criteria are set locally by each chapter — find your community's chapter on the official site. Represents the local/regional scholarship layer, typically far less competitive than national awards and often overlooked

scholarships.name: AFSA National High School Essay Contest
scholarships.eligibility_summary: US high school students in grades 9-12 (public, private, parochial, or home-schooled; parents not in the Foreign Service), writing an essay on a foreign-policy/diplomacy prompt that changes annually; relevant to pre-law/political-science/international-relations interests
